title: carex halleriana subsp. depressa
intro: Carex halleriana subsp. depressa, also known as the small sedge or lesser sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its dense, rounded tufts of dark green leaves and small, greenish-brown flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Carex halleriana subsp. depressa include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, and can also be used as a topical treatment for skin conditions and minor injuries.
